factors.summary: Efficiencies in labor and employee expenses have offset prior gains.

evidence.text: Efficiencies in labor and employee-partner related expense during the six months ended November 30, 2021 offset a prior year gain on the sale of certain operating assets during the six months ended November 30, 2020.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Share buybacks are active, indicating confidence.

evidence.text: On October 29, 2019, we announced the Board of Directors authorized a $1.0 billion share buyback program, which was completed during the first quarter of fiscal 2022. On July 27, 2021, we announced that the Board of Directors authorized a new $1.5 billion share buyback program.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable with active management of existing debt.

evidence.text: The credit agreement that supports our commercial paper and long-term debt markets has historically provided us with sources of liquidity.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is ongoing, particularly in the Uniform Rental and Facility Services and First Aid and Safety Services segments.

evidence.text: Cash paid for acquisitions of businesses was $45.7 million and $6.9 million for the six months ended November 30, 2021 and 2020, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is considered manageable with no material impact.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to other legal proceedings, insurance receipts, legal settlements and claims arising from the ordinary course of its business... the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect.
evidence.subsection: Litigation and Other Contingencies

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business. New business growth resulted from an increase in the number and productivity of sales representatives.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Unit volumes and transaction counts are growing, driven by real demand.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business. New business growth resulted from an increase in the number and productivity of sales representatives.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure.

evidence.text: The increase in expense was primarily due to increases in selling labor and increased travel and meeting expenses.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share through new business and product penetration.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business. New business growth resulted from an increase in the number and productivity of sales representatives.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are stable with the company maintaining its market position.

evidence.text: Cintas is also the creator of the Total Clean Program ™ — a first-of-its-kind service that includes scheduled delivery of essential cleaning supplies, hygienically clean laundering, and sanitizing and disinfecting products and services.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages are strengthening through product innovation and customer relationships.

evidence.text: Cintas is also the creator of the Total Clean Program ™ — a first-of-its-kind service that includes scheduled delivery of essential cleaning supplies, hygienically clean laundering, and sanitizing and disinfecting products and services.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: Customer diversification is improving with a broad range of products and services attracting diverse customer segments.

evidence.text: Cintas has a national sales organization introducing all its products and services to prospects in all market segments. Our broad range of products and services allows our sales organization to consider any type of business a prospect.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are benefiting the company with lower borrowing costs.

evidence.text: Net interest expense (interest expense less interest income) was $21.8 million for the three months ended November 30, 2021, compared to $24.3 million for the three months ended November 30, 2020. The change was primarily due to the replacement of the $250.0 million of senior notes with an interest rate of 4.30% that matured on June 1, 2021, with commercial paper that had an interest rate of 0.22% at November 30, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Demand trends are generally positive, with evidence of both slight organic growth over six months and more significant improvement over three months.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 8.3%. The reportable operating segment’s gross margin was $1,446.9 million, or 47.5% of revenue.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2020

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 9.4% to $1,922.3 million for the three months ended November 30, 2021, compared to $1,757.0 million for the three months ended November 30, 2020.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth has shown mixed trends, with a slight deceleration over the six-month period but an acceleration in the most recent quarter.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 8.3%. The reportable operating segment’s gross margin was $1,446.9 million, or 47.5% of revenue.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2020

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth rate, which adjusts for the impact of acquisitions, divestitures and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, was 9.3%. Revenue growth was negatively impacted by a net 0.2% due to acquisitions and divestitures and positively impacted by 0.3% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly energy and labor costs, which are creating margin pressure. The increase in energy costs contributed to a 40 basis point decline in gross margin.

evidence.text: The change in gross margin from the gross margin of 48.1% for the six months ended November 30, 2020, was primarily due to an increase of 40 basis points in energy costs.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2020

evidence.text: The difference in gross margin as a percent to revenue was driven primarily by a 40 basis point increase in energy costs and labor to generate the revenue growth achieved during the six months ended November 30, 2021 as well as anticipated revenue growth during the remainder of the current fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Gross margins have experienced mixed performance. While there has been a significant improvement in the First Aid and Safety Services segment, overall gross margins are declining due to higher energy and labor costs.

evidence.text: The change in gross margin from the gross margin of 48.1% for the six months ended November 30, 2020, was primarily due to an increase of 40 basis points in energy costs.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2020

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 44.2% for the six months ended November 30, 2021, which was an increase of 270 basis points compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 41.5% in the same period of the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2020

evidence.text: The gross margin was 70 basis points lower than the prior fiscal year’s second quarter gross margin of 47.5%. The difference in gross margin as a percent to revenue was driven primarily by a 40 basis point increase in energy costs and labor to generate the revenue growth achieved during the six months ended November 30, 2021 as well as anticipated revenue growth during the remainder of the current fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: The company's pricing power appears to be mixed. While it has successfully raised prices, contributing to revenue growth, there is evidence that gross margins have slightly declined, potentially indicating some pressure on pricing power.

evidence.text: The change in gross margin from the gross margin of 48.1% for the six months ended November 30, 2020, was primarily due to an increase of 40 basis points in energy costs.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2020

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: Management is maintaining forward guidance, focusing on sustainable growth through strategies that aim to increase revenue across products and services. Despite this, there is no explicit indication of raising or lowering future guidance.

evidence.text: We expect our cash flows from operating activities to remain sufficient to provide us with adequate levels of short-term liquidity.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: Cintas’ principal objective is “to exceed customers’ expectations in order to maximize the long-term value of Cintas for shareholders and working partners,” and it provides the framework and focus for Cintas’ business strategy. This strategy is to achieve revenue growth for all our products and services by increasing our penetration at existing customers and by broadening our customer base to include market segments to which we have not historically served.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Business Strategy

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting, with First Aid and Safety Services experiencing a slight organic decline but a positive impact from acquisitions. Additionally, the revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, which has benefited the company.

evidence.text: First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue increased from $398.9 million to $401.3 million, or 0.6%, for the six months ended November 30, 2021, over the same period in the prior fiscal year. Revenue for this reportable operating segment declined organically by 0.1%.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Six Months Ended November 30, 2020

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 43.5% for the quarter ended November 30, 2021, compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 43.0% in the same period of the prior fiscal year. The improvement in gross margin from the second quarter of the prior year was primarily driven by a decrease in the proportion of sales related to personal protective equipment, which typically have lower gross margins than first aid cabinet sales.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended November 30, 2021 Compared to Three Months Ended November 30, 2020

factors.summary: The economic conditions appear to be mixed. While the overall economic environment is stable, there are lingering risks from the COVID-19 pandemic. However, there are signs of improvement as the rollout of vaccines and lifting of restrictions have positively impacted the business.

evidence.text: The economic environment is stable with potential risks from the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity and Capital Resources

evidence.text: Although the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic is fluid and continues to evolve, we believe our long-term liquidity position remains strong.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations

evidence.text: The on-going roll out of the COVID-19 vaccines and gradual lifting of COVID-19 restrictions had a positive impact on our business during the three and six months ended November 30 2021.
evidence.subsection: Results of Operations
